Q1 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33

The irrigated area under the following crops in a command area is as below:

 
The crop ratio of the area is :
Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)

 
Q2 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

A stream of 150 litres per second was diverted from a canal to irrigate a field. Irrigation was carried out for 6 hours over an area of 2.8 hectares. Due to seepage and evaporation, 0.035 m3 water is lost per second in the stream. The effective depth of root zone is 1.2 m and the available moisture holding capacity of the soil is 18 cm/m. Irrigation was started when the available moisture in the soil was 40%. The total runoff loss from the field was 3 × 105 litres. Select the correct statement(s):

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(a, c)

 
Q3 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

A concrete lined trapezoidal channel has a bed width of 48 m, side slopes of 1.5 H : 1 V, and a depth of flow of 3.2 m. The channel slope is 1 in 5000, and the Manning’s roughness coefficient is 0.014. The discharge (in cumecs, rounded off to nearest integer) is _____ .

NAT
Correct answer: 355
Solution

355 (354 - 356)

 
Q4 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

For an alluvial channel in regime as per Lacey’s theory, which of following statements is correct?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)

 
Q5 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

A channel is to irrigate 6000 hectares of land growing crops.
• Kharif crops : Delta = 50 cm, Kor period = 4 weeks
• Rabi crops : Delta = 70 cm, Kor period = 3 weeks
The discharge of the channel for which it is to be designed is _______ .

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)

 
Q6 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Water is released from a distributary at a constant rate of 2 cumec for 90 days during the total crop period of 120 days. If this discharge irrigates a crop area of 3000 hectares, the nominal duty for this distributary is ______ ha/cumec.

(Answer up to two decimal places)

NAT
Correct answer: 2000
Solution

2000 (1995 - 2005)

 
 
Q7 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

An irrigation channel carries a discharge of 60 cumec and has a critical velocity ratio (CVR) of 1.2. If the critical velocity of flow using Kennedy’s equation is 1.2 m/s, then the depth of flow in the channel is _______ m. (Answer upto three decimal places)

NAT
Correct answer: 2.545
Solution

2.545 (2.535 - 2.555)

 
Q8 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

A head difference of 6 m exists across the floor of a hydraulic structure. The soil below the floor has a specific gravity of 2.65 and a void ratio of 1.7. Shape factor for the hydraulic structure is 2.44. Using Khosla’s theory and assuming safety against piping, the minimum horizontal length of the floor is ______ m. (Answer upto two decimal places)

NAT
Correct answer: 7.50
Solution

7.50 (7 - 8)

 
Q9 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

A field of 1200 m2 is irrigated to raise the soil moisture content from 10% to field capacity. The dry bulk density of the soil 1450 kg/m3 and the root zone depth is 1.5 m. A total of 720 m3 of water is applied, out of which 12% is lost due to deep percolation and evaporation. The field capacity of the soil is :

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)

 
Q10 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

In a gravity dam with a drainage gallery, the tail water depth present is ‘h’. The height of the water from the base of the reservoir at upstream side is ‘H’. The uplift pressure distribution is modified due to the presence of gallery. According to USBR recommendations, the uplift pressure at the gallery location is taken as:

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)

 
Q11 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

A wind of 85 km/hr blows over a reservoir towards a dam. From a point on the edge of reservoir,
the longest straight line distance to the dam is 38 km, and there is no obstruction in between.
Assume the wind acts uniformly over this length and generates waves that impact the dam face.
The total force due to wave action per unit length acting on the dam is ____ kN/m. The pressure
distribution may be assumed to be triangular with height as  times the height of wave. (Take = 9.81 kN/m3.)

(Answer upto two decimal places)

NAT
Correct answer: 65
Solution

65 (64.5 - 66.5)

 
 
Q12 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

The depth of water penetration at five equidistant points along a field were recorded as 1.7 m, 1.6 m, 1.4 m, 1.2 m and 1.1 m. The water distribution efficiency (in percent; rounded off to one decimal place) is _______ .

NAT
Correct answer: 85.7
Solution

85.7 (85.6 - 85.8)

 
Q13 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Among the following statements related to the elementary profile gravity dams and hydraulic structures, the incorrect statement(s) is/are:

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(b,d)

 
 
Q14 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

A reservoir is planned to serve a cultivable command area of 80,000 hectares. The cropping pattern and relevant data are given below:

 
The losses in the canal and reservoir are 6% and 12% respectively. The gross storage capacity required for the reservoir is _______ ha-m.
Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)

 
Q15 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

A soil has a field capacity of 32% and a permanent wilting point of 10%. The bulk density is 1280 kg/m3 and the effective root zone depth is 700 mm. The daily consumptive use of water by the crop is 10 mm. To avoid stress, irrigation is scheduled such that the moisture content must not fall below 25% of the water holding capacity between the field capacity and the permanent
wilting point. The irrigation interval is _____ days. (Report your answer as an integer).

NAT
Correct answer: 14
Solution

14 (13 - 15)

A water sample has the following concentrations:

 
Based on this data, the water is classified as :
Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)

 
Q17 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

A gravity dam has a downstream slope of 0.7 H : 1 V. The vertical stress at the toe is 3300 kN/m2. The water pressure on the downstream end when tail water is present is 75 kN/m2. The major principal stresses at the toe when tail water is present and tail water is absent, respectively, are :

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
